channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Steve Bannon's trial in a border wall charity scheme case delayed until February
item.description: Conservative strategist Steve Bannon’s trial on state charges of conspiring to dupe donors to a border wall charity will be postponed from next month to late February.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump signals use of military for deportations
item.description: President-elect Trump recirculated a social media post Monday suggesting his incoming administration will declare an immigration national emergency and use military assets to support his mass deportation pledge, labeling the claim “TRUE!!!” On his Truth Social site, Trump at 4:03 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal workers brace for Trump overhaul of civil service
item.description: President-elect Trump’s plans to reshape the federal workforce would allow him to fire wide swaths of career employees and replace them with political appointees — a move that comes as he has demanded loyalty at every level of government.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Senegal's ruling party poised for parliamentary majority in boost for reform agenda
item.description: Senegal president's party PASTEF is poised to win a majority of seats in the National Assembly as main opposition parties conceded defeat Monday in the country's parliamentary elections.
item.dc:creator: Mark Banchereau, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A West Texas lawmaker wants to redirect millions of tax dollars to plug abandoned wells, curb emissions
item.description: The proposal comes after the state’s oil and gas regulatory agency said it needs more money to plug wells that are in some instances erupting.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Carlos Nogueras Ramos, KPRC 2

item.title: Pentagon fails 7th audit in a row but says progress made
item.description: The nation's largest government agency is unable to fully account for its more than $824 billion budget.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, NewsNation

item.title: Pentagon bracing for sweeping changes after Trump nominates Pete Hegseth for secretary
item.description: The Pentagon is bracing for sweeping policy changes under the incoming Trump administration, and some high-level officers could find their careers on the chopping block.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaetz-gate: Navigating the President-elect's most baffling Cabinet pick
item.description: While former Rep. Matt Gaetz, R.-Fla., may be out of Congress, Congress certainly isn't done with him – and all eyes are on the report the House Ethics Committee is sitting on.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump taps FCC commissioner Brendan Carr to chair the agency
item.description: Trump said he first nominated Carr to the FCC in 2017 and he was confirmed unanimously by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's Pentagon pick paid woman after sex assault allegation but denies wrongdoing, his lawyer says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pete Hegseth, President-elect Donald Trump's pick for defense secretary, paid a woman who accused him of sexual assault to head off the threat of a baseless lawsuit, according to Hegseth’s lawyer.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US long-range missiles to strike inside Russia
item.description: President Biden has approved Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's regime to strike targets within Russia using American long-range missiles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big voter turnout this year benefited Republicans, contradicting conventional political wisdom
item.description: The 2024 presidential election featured sky-high turnout, approaching the historic levels of the 2020 contest and contradicting long-held conventional political wisdom that Republicans struggle to win races in which many people vote.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump attends UFC championship fight in New York, taking a break from Cabinet picks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump walked out to a roaring standing ovation just ahead of the start of the UFC pay-per-view card at Madison Square Garden on Saturday night, combining two things close to his heart: fierce battles inside the octagon and New York City.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In a meeting with Biden, China's Xi cautions US to 'make the wise choice' to keep relations stable
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — China’s leader Xi Jinping met for the last time with U.S. President Joe Biden on Saturday but was already looking ahead to President-elect Donald Trump and his “America first” policies, saying Beijing “is ready to work with a new U.S. administration.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names attorney Will Scharf to key White House job
item.description: Scharf represented Trump as one of his lawyers during a hearing on presidential immunity before the Supreme Court and was a regular on television defending Trump amid his hush money trial in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Lara Trump emerges as an early favorite to fill Rubio's Senate seat
item.description: Appointing Lara Trump would ensure that the incoming president has a loyalist in the Senate, but it would most definitely come with criticism from Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: UAP whistleblower says they need greater protections
item.description: Whistleblowers testified at a House of Representatives committee this week regarding the existence of UAPs or UAP-related programs.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Women stock up on abortion pills and Plan B, fearing new restrictions under Trump
item.description: Telehealth providers say requests for the pills have spiked since the election. Patients and doctors worry what a Trump presidency could mean for medical abortion and emergency contraception.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Boden and Elissa Nadworny, MPR News

item.title: Hubbard County election judge faces felony charges for allegedly allowing unregistered voters to cast ballots
item.description: Prosecutors in Hubbard County say Timothy Michael Scouton — who was head judge at a precinct in rural Badoura Township — allowed 11 people to vote even though they had not registered.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: What’s ahead for abortion access in Minnesota under a Trump administration
item.description: While detailed plans have yet to emerge, health and legal experts, practitioners and advocates in Minnesota are closely monitoring the new administration for hints about what federal changes to abortion could mean for the state.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's emerging Cabinet
item.description: President-elect Trump has made quick work of pulling together a Cabinet and group of senior aides for his incoming administration, roiling Washington with some of his more controversial picks in the process.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Clark County finds 1,600 uncounted mail-in ballots in secure vault
item.description: Clark County elections officials discovered more than 1,600 uncounted mail-in ballots Thursday, more than a week after Election Day, a spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The last actions the Biden administration will take before Trump takes over the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Biden administration officials are working against the clock doling out billions in grants and taking other steps to try to preserve at least some of the outgoing president’s legacy before President-elect Donald Trump takes office in January.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, MATTHEW DALY and COLLIN BINKLEY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Appeals court allows Indiana gender-affirming care ban to remain in effect
item.description: Indiana’s Republican Gov. Eric Holcomb signed the law, Senate Bill 480, last spring, one day after he called the measure “clear as mud.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Florida GOP chair approves Gaetz AG nomination; state democratic chair concerned
item.description: There are increasing questions from political figures surrounding President-elect Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Justice Department. However, former Rep. Matt Gaetz is getting support from Evan Power, the chair of the Florida GOP.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Two Carlton County races end in ties — winners determined by chance
item.description: The race for Mayor in the city of Wrenshall, and for one of two city council seats in Kettle River, both ended in dead heats. According to state statute, an election that ends in a tie is decided by lot.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Jacky Rosen looks forward to 2nd term with focus on economy; Nevada senator strongly opposes mass deportation
item.description: Democratic Nevada Sen. Jacky Rosen plans to focus on the economy and kitchen-table issues after voters re-elected her to a second term in Congress, she told 8 News Now.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What can Florida expect from the next legislative session
item.description: Florida legislature meets for an organization session 14 days after the general election to swear in new members and speak on what the year ahead could look like at the capitol.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Thune elected Senate majority leader
item.description: Sen. John Thune (R-S.D.) has been elected Senate majority leader, setting the stage for him to replace retiring Sen. Mitch McConnell (Ky.), who has held the top Senate GOP leadership job for the past 18 years. Thune has served as Senate Republican whip, the No.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Elon Musk, Vivek Ramaswamy land Trump administration roles
item.description: President-elect Trump has tapped tech entrepreneurs Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy to lead an advisory group focused on cutting federal spending.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, PIX 11
